headline: Sabah Squatter Settlements House 380,000 Residents
context: State survey identified 380,000 people in 850 squatter settlements across Sabah predominantly in Kota Kinabalu and Sandakan.

cards.big: 380,000 people live in 850 squatter settlements across Sabah
cards.sub: Nearly 10% of state population lacks formal housing or land tenure

cards.big: KK alone has 280 squatter settlements on state and private land
cards.sub: Pulau Gaya water village houses 15,000 residents in fire-prone structures

cards.big: State demolished 45 settlements in 2025 displacing 12,000 residents
cards.sub: Only 2,800 displaced households received alternative housing or relocation aid

cards.big: 68% of squatter residents are undocumented or stateless persons
cards.sub: Remaining 32% are citizens unable to afford formal housing in urban areas

cards.big: Squatter prevalence tests whether housing policy reaches the most vulnerable populations
380,000 squatter residents in Sabah — regularisation is cheaper and more humane than demolition cycles.
